instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/investments/apps-in-your-pocket.html
Apps In Your Pocket
Besides social networking apps like FB, Twitter, LinkedIn and Instagram which I must be accessing a zillion times daily, my number 1 app for K_I_T (keeping in touch) is Whatsapp not only for personal communication but also official communication between office groups, vendors, inter department file transfers etc. Paytm is the next app on my list since it has made life truly worry-free right from paying my utility bills to shopping online, booking bus/flight tickets and also sending money to my loved ones. OLA is next, primarily since it helps ensure I am never stranded for transportation when I am travelling anyplace/ returning from the airport etc. Google Maps is another favourite since it helps getting me to my destination, altering me of traffic conditions, and ensuring I don’t have to look around for a paanwalabhaiya/ taxi wala to tell me an address I don’t know.
What are your 4 most frequently used apps?
A new app you would highly recommend?The Hotstar app is the best entertainment application I have experienced recently for watching the latest released movies, live sports, cricket and Television shows which I highly recommend. Being from the BFSI sector, Expense Manager is another app I would recommend you should have on your phone. It is an easy tool to keep a track of all your monthly expenses. It helps you schedule payments make recurring payments too. Expense Manager even helps you scan your spending behaviour to help cut down on any unnecessary expenses.
How do you evaluate an app before downloading it?I see the app size, number of times the app has been downloaded, the ratings, latest comments and responses given by the app developer acknowledging lapses/ solutions to make it work. A mix of these elements helps me decide before downloading the app.
An app that you envision would be useful in the future?That would depend on my individual need, may it be travel, entertainment, banking, food ordering etc. Today, people are looking at a singular interface for all their requirements instead of multiple platforms. Apps like Google today are already adapting to become a single platform for servicing the customer for any of his needs.
Profile:Ranjan Dutt is a thorough bred sales and marketing professional with substantial exposure and experience spanning across global FMCG giants like Coca Cola India, strong IT/ tech and telecom giants like Satyam Infoway and Idea Cellular, versatile Life Insurance brands like Bajaj Life Insurance Limited, ICICI Prudential and TATA AIA Life across geography, culture and functional expertise. High on integrity, an energetic leader known for his ability to envision and create successful outcomes in complex situations and 100% commitment to drive sales, profit and market share growth over the last 19+ years.
He is presently responsible for Brand Strategy, Digital Marketing & Transformation, Channel Marketing, Corporate Communication and Market Research for Bajaj Life Insurance Limited.
He is recognized as an innovative & creative Life insurance Sales & Marketing professional who leverages the use of Social Media & technology in propagating Life Insurance sales & recruitment. I carry the firm belief that the future of Sales & Marketing revolves around the Digital World both in terms of using the internet & social media as well as technology to create a smart & savvy distribution. In my limited spare time, I blog on topics related to Life Insurance Sales, Team Building, Leadership, Goal Setting & Product Marketing.
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility
*The Guaranteed benefits are dependant on the policy terms, sum assured, premium and age along with other variable factors . For more details please refer to sales brochure.
---------------------------------------------------------
